topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the locker room was a heavy, suffocating blanket. Leo stared at the polished floor, the reflection of his own strained face mocking him. He had poured every ounce of his being into those grueling practices, the early mornings, the relentless conditioning, all for this moment. Now, the names were being called, and his name wasn't among them. A slow, cold knot tightened in his stomach, spreading outward until his chest felt constricted, like a vice grip. He forced a smile, a brittle thing that didn't reach his eyes, and mumbled a quick, inadequate response. Every glance from his teammates felt like a spotlight, illuminating his failure. He wanted to bolt, to find anywhere else, but his feet felt rooted to the cold tile.

[story 2]

"Well, that's that, Leo," Coach barked, his voice sharp enough to cut glass. The words landed like physical blows. I stood there, my hands clenched so tightly my knuckles were white islands against my skin. I watched the other hopefuls gather their gear, their laughter echoing in the vast space. A dull ache pulsed behind my eyes, a throbbing reminder of the wasted effort, the sheer futility of it all. I kept my gaze fixed on the worn laces of my boots, tracing the frayed threads with my eyes, trying to find some misplaced piece of logic, some overlooked error in my performance that would explain this exclusion. The air felt thick, resistant to my breath.

[story 3]

The team meeting felt like wading through deep, viscous water. I sat at the edge of the bench, the wood digging uncomfortably into my thighs. When the final roster was announced, the sound seemed distant, muffled. A strange numbness washed over me, a complete shutdown. I didn't move. My jaw was tight, a hard line set across my face. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush of humiliation so intense it burned. I looked up when the coach finished, meeting his eyes for a fraction of a second before dropping my gaze again. The expectation that had fueled me for so long had evaporated, leaving behind only a hollow echo in my ears.
